task.title: Character-scoped canonical recall in group conversations
task.source: Direct maintainer report reproduced on the Pi
task.classification: prompt assembly and reusable generation context

coreClaim: Each generated group-conversation reply recalls active-chat canonical memories plus only the answering character's character-scoped canonical memories.

claimBoundary.coreClaim: A targeted group reply sees the active chat scope and its own character scope; changing forCharacterId invalidates target-sensitive reusable context.
claimBoundary.riskType: prompt-context ownership and cross-character data isolation
entrypoints: Consecutive same-send group replies using PromptAssemblyReusableContext
currentPathsOrFormats: canonical-memories rows with chat, scene, or character scope
legacyPathsOrFormats: Single-character prompts without a target keep their existing participant scope
Merged or untargeted generation keeps the existing participant set
Chat-scoped and scene-scoped memories remain eligible independently of character scope
outOfScopeClaims: This PR does not reduce character-card prompt size.
This PR does not remove intentional group-turn guidance.
This PR does not rewrite existing saved prompt snapshots.

contractLaneGate.brokenContract: Prompt assembly queried canonical memories for every attached character and reused the first reply's target-sensitive context for later group replies.
contractLaneGate.consumer: canonical-memory retrieval and provider-ready group reply prompts
contractLaneGate.impliedContract: A per-character reply may use shared active-chat context and the answering character's durable card memory without inheriting another participant's private character scope.
contractLaneGate.actualEnforcement: Prompt assembly derives the effective conversation or roleplay group target, selects that character for canonical-memory queries, and includes the same target in reusable-context matching.
contractLaneGate.primaryOwnerDetail: Prompt assembly owns generation targets, prompt-context selection, and reusable-context validity.
contractLaneGate.wrongLaneFixToAvoid: Do not filter attribution in the UI, mutate stored memory scopes, or add provider-side prompt rewriting.
regressionProof: canonical-memory-context.spec.ts assembles Jester then Harlequin with one reusable context and proves chat-plus-owner-only recall
The same test proves the second result reorders prompt characters to Harlequin instead of retaining Jester
Focused prompt suites and pnpm check pass

positiveRows.claim: Jester receives the active-chat memory and Jester's character memory.
positiveRows.evidence: The focused regression asserts memory-chat and memory-jester in Jester's canonical attribution.

positiveRows.claim: Harlequin receives the active-chat memory and Harlequin's character memory after reusing Jester's prompt context.
positiveRows.evidence: The focused regression passes reusableContext into Harlequin's assembly and asserts memory-chat, memory-harlequin, and Harlequin as the first prompt character.
Four focused prompt files pass 50 tests; pnpm typecheck, pnpm check:architecture, and pnpm check exit successfully.

contradictionRows.claim: Jester must not receive Harlequin's character-scoped memory.
contradictionRows.evidence: The regression failed on original main because memory-harlequin was present, then passed after the owner-level fix.

contradictionRows.claim: Harlequin must not reuse Jester's prompt target or Jester's character-scoped memory.
contradictionRows.evidence: The regression failed on original main with Jester still first and memory-jester still attributed, then passed after target-sensitive invalidation.

legacyDefaultRows.claim: Active chat memories remain available to every targeted group reply.
legacyDefaultRows.evidence: Both Jester and Harlequin assertions retain memory-chat.

legacyDefaultRows.claim: Untargeted generation preserves the existing participant-set behavior.
legacyDefaultRows.evidence: Production filtering is conditional on a valid scoped conversation or roleplay target; existing canonical-memory tests pass unchanged.

untestedRows: Existing saved prompt snapshots remain historical and are not recomputed.
The final Pi image and runtime behavior are verified after merge and deployment.

ownedFacts.description: The reported Pi prompt snapshots gave Jester and Harlequin the same ten canonical memory ids drawn from all three character scopes.
ownedFacts.evidence: The selected Hey, guys chat showed identical ten-item attribution; canonical-memories storage confirmed those rows were separately scoped to Jester, Harlequin, and Pierrot.

ownedFacts.description: The prompt-size warning is dominated by character-card context rather than recalled memories.
ownedFacts.evidence: Jester's saved prompt estimated 9,370 tokens with about 9,340 in Character Context and about 418 tokens in the Canonical Memory section.

negativeRowsTested: Target change invalidates reusable prompt-character order

notes: The maintainer explicitly authorized PR creation, merge, and Pi deployment after hosted gates pass.
The first hosted Bunny receipt saw superseded draft-transition cancellations alongside the successful final-head CI run, so a clean-head evidence commit and Bunny retry are required before merge.
